In compliance with Department of Labor requirements for employers petitioning for H-1B/E-3 status, this
notice lists the position for which Emory University intends to hire an H-1B/E-3 employee. Candidates
have already been selected to fill the positions. The "Notice of Filing of H-1B/E-3 Labor Condition
Application" is not a posting for an open position.
Complaints alleging misrepresentation of material facts in the labor condition application and/or failure to
comply with the terms of the labor condition application may be filed with any office of the Wage and
Hour division of the United States Department of Labor.
The labor condition application is available for public inspection at Emory University International Student
and Scholar Services, 1784 North Decatur Road, Suite 130, Atlanta, Georgia, 30322.

rawText:
NOTICE OF LCA FILING
Case Invoice #: 00002026025

Notice of the Filing of a Labor Condition Application with the Employment and Training Administration.
1. An H-1B nonimmigrant worker is being sought by the University of Kansas Medical Center
through the filing of a Labor Condition Application (Form 9035) with the Employment and
Training Administration of the U.S. Department of Labor.
2. One such worker is being sought.
3. This worker is being sought as a Physicist/Faculty.
4. An annual wage of $210,000 is being offered to this worker.
5. The period of employment for which this worker is sought is from 07/06/2026 to 07/05/2029.
6. The employment will occur at 3901 Rainbow Blvd., Kansas City, KS 66160; 4001 Rainbow Blvd.,
Kansas City, KS 66160; 4000 Cambridge St., Kansas City, KS 66160; 3825 Cambridge St., Kansas
City, KS 66103; and 15123 S. OMC Parkway, Olathe, KS 66061.
7. The Labor Condition Application is available for public inspection at the offices of 3901 Rainbow
Blvd., Kansas City, KS 66160.
“Complaints alleging misrepresentation of material facts in the labor condition application and/or failure
to comply with the terms of the Labor Condition Application may be filed with any office of the Wage
and Hour Division of the United States Department of Labor.”
This is to certify that the above notice was posted for 10 consecutive business days from:
_________________ to ______________________.

_______________________________
Stacie Rader
Program Manager

Notice is hereby given that the University of Pittsburgh, School of Medicine intends to file a
Labor Condition Application (LCA) with the U.S. Department of Labor indicating its
intention to seek ONE H-1B non-immigrant(s) for the position of:
Assistant Professor – Molecular Genomic Pathology

locations:

OFFICE OF INTERNATIONAL SERVICES
University Center for International Studies
University of Pittsburgh

NOTICE OF FILING OF A
LABOR CONDITION APPLICATION
________________________________________________________________

Notice is hereby given that the University of Pittsburgh, School of Medicine intends to file a
Labor Condition Application (LCA) with the U.S. Department of Labor indicating its
intention to seek ONE H-1B non-immigrant(s) for the position of:
Assistant Professor - Adult Cardiology

With validity dates from 9/1/2026 to 8/31/2029
The salary for this position is $156.25 per hour. The employment will occur at the following
locations:
5230 Centre Avenue, Pittsburgh, PA 15232
2775 Mosside Boulevard, Monroeville, PA 15146
200 Lothrop Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15213
9100 Babcock Boulevard, Pittsburgh, PA 15237
1 St. Francis Way, Cranberry Township, PA 16066
1400 Locust Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15219
1500 Fifth Avenue, McKeesport, PA 15132
700 High Street, Williamsport, PA 17701
5832 PA-981, Latrobe, PA 15650
664 Chilliwack Lane, Mars, PA 16046
The Labor Condition Application will be available for inspection in:
Office of International Services, Room 708 William Pitt Union, Pittsburgh, PA 15260
Complaints alleging misrepresentation of material facts in the Labor Condition Application
and/or failure to comply with the terms of the Labor Condition Application may be filed with
any office of the Wage Hour Division of the United States Department of Labor.

rawText:
NOTICE OF LCA FILING
Case # [KBGOVI07995-2]

Notice of the Filing of a Labor Condition Application with the Employment and Training
Administration.
1. An H-1B nonimmigrant worker is being sought by the University of Kansas Medical
Center through the filing of a Labor Condition Application (Form 9035) with the
Employment and Training Administration of the U.S. Department of Labor.
2. One such worker is being sought.
3. This worker is being sought as a Pulmonary and Critical Care Nocturnist
Physician/Faculty.
4. A salary of $435,000 is being offered to this worker.
5. The period of employment for which this worker is sought is from 13 Jul 2026 to
12 Jul 2029.
6. The employment will occur at 3901 Rainbow Blvd., Kansas City, KS 66160; 4000
Cambridge St., Kansas City, KS 66160; 2000 Olathe Blvd., Kansas City, KS 66160; and
20333 W. 151st St., Olathe, KS 66061.
7. The Labor Condition Application is available for public inspection at the main campus
address - 3901 Rainbow Blvd., Kansas City, KS 66160.
“Complaints alleging misrepresentation of material facts in the labor condition application
and/or failure to comply with the terms of the Labor Condition Application may be filed with
any office of the Wage and Hour Division of the United States Department of Labor.”
This is to certify that the above notice was posted for 10 consecutive business days from:
_________________ to ______________________.
_______________________________
Stacie Rader
Program Manager
